description_long: This is a large, multi-tiered pagoda structure with five distinct stories, each separated by wide, overhanging eaves that curve upward at the corners. The building is primarily constructed from wood, with dark gray tiled roofs and golden-brown structural elements, evoking traditional East Asian architecture. The base is rectangular and surrounded by a low boundary, possibly representing a stone or concrete walkway. The facade features regularly spaced columns and railings, with ornamental details along the roof edges. The main entrance is centrally located on the front side, marked by a pair of green doors or panels. The overall design is symmetrical, with a strong emphasis on horizontal layering and decorative rooflines.
description: A grand, five-story East Asian pagoda with upward-curving eaves, dark tiled roofs, and golden-brown wooden details, set on a rectangular base with a surrounding walkway.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the main entrance, symmetrical facade, and all five roof tiers with prominent upward-curving eaves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the layered roofs, and the rectangular base with visible boundary lines.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, but without a visible entrance, showing the full height and tiered roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with the rectangular base and layered roofs visible, and some greenery near the base.
